behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: AI judgment-quality error (the draft is fluent but generic and misses thread-specific points). Required setup: run generation with a real LLM on real thread content and score grounding to thread-specific points. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed model can be made to always include points and would hide real grounding failures. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text covers only generation quality for this step, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed set of real email threads with known key points, generate drafts via the production LLM, and assert each draft cites required thread-specific details above a grounding threshold (repeat runs to detect variability).

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between draft-claim extraction, KB lookup, and contradiction flagging. Required setup: wire real checker modules together against a test KB and drafted claims. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) behavior is objective contradiction to KB entries, so correctness need not depend on non-deterministic model judgment; (c) no multi-agentic chain required. Rejected higher level (Agentic/System): behavior text says 'contradict entries in the knowledge base' (deterministic consistency target), not subjective LLM-quality evaluation or real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Run checker pipeline in-process with seeded KB facts and drafts containing explicit contradictory claims; assert contradiction flags, offending claim spans, and KB evidence IDs are produced correctly.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: AI judgment-quality error under missing-context conditions (hallucination vs disclaimer behavior). Required setup: retrieval returns no relevant KB entries, then real LLM draft generation is evaluated for mandated disclaimer and absence of fabricated facts. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs would not expose hallucination tendency. Rejected higher level (Workflow): this behavior targets one generation decision conditioned on retrieval-empty state, not a full multi-agentic pipeline assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Create prompts from threads where KB retrieval is intentionally empty, generate drafts with production LLM, and assert disclaimer presence plus no unsupported factual claims across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module orchestration/wiring error between approval state machine and send action. Required setup: exercise approval + send modules together with a fake mail sender and verify send is blocked pre-approval. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed to expose gating bug; (b) no AI-output dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain requirement. Rejected higher level (System): behavior is control-flow gating and can fail entirely within in-process module interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate draft creation and presentation, attempt send before approval, assert zero send calls/events; then record explicit approval and assert exactly one send call is emitted.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: multi-step orchestration error across sequential agentic and non-agentic stages (thread ingestion -> LLM draft -> fact check -> approval gating). Required setup: real end-to-end run on a real email thread with production sequencing and real model call(s). MULTI-STEP applies directly: multiple steps in sequence with at least one real agentic generation step, so minimum is Workflow. Rejected lower levels: behavior text explicitly requires 'full sequence ... end-to-end for a real email thread,' which disqualifies isolated integration/system-only tests.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production pipeline on a real thread fixture/source, verify step outputs and handoffs at each stage, require human-approval gate before send, and confirm final outcome matches expected end-to-end behavior.
